checks.finding: REQ-001 through REQ-005 define observable shell, HMAC, denial, adversarial-data, isolation, and PowerShell-parity outcomes, with planned TEST-001 through TEST-005.
REQ-001 maps to AC-001; REQ-002 to AC-002 and AC-003; REQ-003 to AC-004; and REQ-004 plus REQ-005 to AC-005.
AC-001 through AC-005 specify inspectable code-boundary, valid-token, tampering-denial, adversarial-payload, fixture-isolation, and parity outcomes.
The non-goals explicitly preserve token semantics and exclude panelist execution, sanitization, evidence contracts, consensus changes, UI, network calls, and persistent storage.

The specification fixes the quoted-heredoc/environment-data boundary, canonical HMAC-SHA256 message, constant-time comparison, fail-closed behavior, no-secret/no-network fixtures, and PowerShell parity constraint.

The critical code-execution and invalid-consent risks have planned validation through real-HMAC acceptance, tampering denial, adversarial operand testing, static boundary inspection, and PowerShell parity coverage.
